summary: Iter21 (Channel Health widget + Phase 2/3/4 integrations: FB Page picker, Meta Ads bind, Hunter bind, Admin Platform Setup, Razorpay live-key swap) — 25/25 backend pytest pass. Frontend: ChannelHealthWidget renders on /dashboard, /connect renders with Meta Ads + Hunter forms, /admin/platform-setup loads with 3 providers + 5 services. Lead enrichment AI-only fallback works without Hunter binding. No token leaks, no _id leaks, admin auth gating correct.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now 7114 lines — strongly recommend splitting integrations/admin/leads/copilot into separate routers/modules. File is becoming unmaintainable.

/api/integrations/health 'meta_ads' message text contains 'META_ACCESS_TOKEN' literal env-var name. Functionally fine but a naive grep-for-'access_token' security scan will flag it as a false positive — consider rewording the help text (e.g. 'set the Meta access-token env var').
/integrations/meta-ads/bind: the access_token length check (<30) is loose — real Meta system-user tokens start with 'EAA' and are ~200+ chars. Could enforce startswith('EAA') for tighter validation.
/integrations/hunter/bind: real Hunter API keys are 40 lowercase hex chars; current >=20 length check is loose.

/admin/razorpay/swap-live: writes os.environ['RAZORPAY_KEY_ID'] in-process — works for the current pod but won't propagate to other replicas behind ingress until they restart and read platform_config from mongo. Confirm startup loader at line ~7050 reads platform_config doc and overrides env on each pod boot.

/leads/{id}/enrich uses lead.first_name / lead.last_name in the Hunter email-finder request, but /api/leads stores 'name' as a single field (per iter20 context). Hunter email-finder will silently get empty params — not a crash but wasted Hunter quota.

success_rate.frontend: 100% — channel-health-widget on /dashboard, connect-page renders, admin-platform-pending banner shows for admin, meta-ads-bind-form opens & rejects bad input via 400, hunter-bind-form opens & rejects bad key via 400, admin-platform-setup loads with 3 provider cards + 5 service cards, non-admin gets 403 on /api/admin/platform-setup (verified directly)

test_credentials: admin@zeromark.ai / admin123 (read from /app/memory/test_credentials.md). New non-admin test users created on the fly with email TEST_iter21_*@example.com.
seed_data_creation: Per pytest run: 1 fresh non-admin test user (TEST_iter21_<uuid>@example.com), 1 lead (TEST Iter21 Lead), Meta Ads + Hunter integrations bound/unbound idempotently. No persistent seed pollution since user is sandboxed by workspace_id.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Important data-testids: channel-health-widget (Dashboard), connect-page, admin-platform-pending, admin-setup-link, channel-{linkedin|twitter|facebook|instagram|meta_ads}, status-{id}, meta-ads-bind-open, meta-ads-bind-form, meta-ads-token, meta-ads-acct, meta-ads-submit, meta-ads-disconnect, hunter-card, hunter-bind-open, hunter-bind-form, hunter-key, hunter-submit, hunter-disconnect, fb-page-picker, fb-page-{id}, admin-platform-setup, provider-{linkedin|twitter|meta}, service-{groq|gmail|twilio|razorpay|fernet}, copy-callback-{id}, open-portal-{id}. Backend endpoint paths verified: GET /api/integrations/health, GET/POST /api/integrations/facebook/{pages,select-page}, POST/DELETE /api/integrations/meta-ads, POST/DELETE /api/integrations/hunter, GET /api/admin/platform-setup, POST /api/admin/razorpay/swap-live, POST /api/leads/{id}/enrich (NOTE: actual path is /enrich, NOT /enrich-domain as referenced in the review request). Login redirects admin to /admin (not /dashboard) — to verify dashboard widgets, navigate explicitly to /dashboard.
rca of the issue: No issues blocking the iteration. One clarification: in this preview env LINKEDIN_CLIENT_ID / TWITTER_CLIENT_ID / FACEBOOK_APP_ID are intentionally NOT set, so /integrations/health correctly returns provider_configured=false for those 4 OAuth channels and the Connect page shows 'Awaiting platform setup' pills + the admin-only banner — this is the intended state per the review request. Once the platform owner registers the real Meta/LinkedIn/X dev apps and sets the env vars (visible on /admin/platform-setup), every customer can OAuth in 30 sec.
